medications: Calcium carbonate with vitamin D3 daily
Topical diclofenac gel as needed for knee/hand pain
health.mental_health: Keeps significant parts of her personal life hidden within a conservative faith and diaspora circle, leading to chronic stress, sleep disruption, and periodic anxiety about being outed; she uses an LGBTQ-affirming counselor discreetly and avoids putting details on insurance paperwork when possible.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for blood pressure management (January)
DEXA scan review and osteoporosis medication refill (February)
Physical therapy evaluation for knee osteoarthritis and fall-prevention exercises (late February)
Dental checkup to monitor jaw health while on bisphosphonate therapy (March)

finance.debt: Remaining balance on a 15-year mortgage for her condo in Northgate; small revolving credit card balance that spikes during family travel and medical copays.
investments: High-yield savings account for emergency fund
recent_transactions: Monthly HOA dues and condo mortgage payment
Copay for DEXA scan and primary care visit
Discreet counseling sessions paid out-of-pocket to avoid insurance statements
Flight purchase to visit extended family in Kent and Tukwila for a church fundraiser weekend

relationships: Older sister, Wanjiku, in Kent—close but very conservative; Aisha is careful about what she shares
Church community in Rainier Valley—active volunteer but guarded about personal life
Longtime friend and confidante, Marisol Reyes, in Capitol Hill—one of the few people who knows her sexual orientation
A private, on-and-off relationship with a woman she met through a small LGBTQ book club; they avoid public outings near her church network
social.social_media_activity: Uses Facebook mostly for church announcements, family photos, and community events; keeps posts neutral and avoids tagging or being tagged at LGBTQ venues. Uses WhatsApp heavily with family groups and church committees; keeps a separate, locked email and calendar for personal matters.
private_conversations: Discussing boundaries and safety around being outed within her church and family circles
Talking with her counselor about loneliness, identity, and fear of community backlash
Quietly asking Marisol for recommendations for LGBTQ-friendly healthcare providers and legal planning
Negotiating with her partner about attending public events and travel without being seen by acquaintances

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM–5:00 PM (hybrid: in office Tue–Thu)
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check-in at a local pharmacy kiosk every other Saturday
Physical therapy exercises at home 4x/week; in-person PT every other Wednesday at 6:00 PM
Monthly counseling session (private-pay) on a Friday afternoon, scheduled as a 'personal appointment'
Quarterly osteoporosis follow-up labs as ordered by primary care
upcoming_events: HR compliance training facilitation (next Tuesday, 10:00 AM)
Church committee meeting in Rainier Valley (Sunday, 1:00 PM)
DEXA follow-up call with clinician (two weeks from now, 4:30 PM)
Small LGBTQ book club meeting in Capitol Hill (next Thursday evening—kept off her shared calendars)
schedule.daily_routine: Starts the day with a short walk when her knees allow, checks blood pressure a few times a week, works a structured HR day with frequent desk time, and tries to do PT stretches in the evening; she keeps personal plans separate from church and family commitments to avoid questions.

professional.company: Mid-size healthcare technology company in South Lake Union
projects: Updating employee handbook sections on leave, accommodations, and remote work
Coordinating annual open enrollment and benefits communications
Supporting a manager training series on performance documentation and respectful workplace conduct
Assisting with a pay equity review and job description standardization
professional.performance_issues: Generally strong performer, but she occasionally avoids leading certain DEI discussions in front of colleagues who are connected to her faith community, worried about rumors; she has also taken discreet time off for medical and counseling appointments and is careful about how it appears on shared calendars.
workplace_relationships: Trusted working relationship with her direct manager, Priya Shah, who values her discretion and accuracy
Friendly but cautious dynamic with a colleague who attends the same church network; Aisha keeps conversations strictly work-focused
Occasional tension with a department head who pushes back on accommodation requests; Aisha documents everything carefully

hobbies: Water aerobics at a community center (joint-friendly exercise)
Gardening on her condo balcony (herbs and tomatoes)
Weekend walks at Green Lake when pain is manageable
habits: Tracks blood pressure and salt intake most days
Limits alcohol at church and family events to avoid scrutiny; occasionally has a glass of wine privately at home
Keeps two phone lock screens and uses separate contact names to reduce the chance of a partner’s messages being seen
Avoids being photographed at certain gatherings and declines invitations that could expose her personal life
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a two-bedroom condo in Northgate; she presents it to her conservative community as practical for guests and family visits, while privately valuing the autonomy and privacy it provides.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked, lower-sodium diet for hypertension; emphasizes leafy greens, beans, fish, and calcium-rich foods; reduces sugary snacks but keeps ginger tea and spicy foods as comfort staples.
